title: lotoxalis glabrata
intro: Lotoxalis glabrata, also known as the yellow or orange wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the oxalis genus. It is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Lotoxalis glabrata include its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic and diuretic, which can be achieved through herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions.
